# Society & Economics — 2008

Notable events, milestones, and developments in society and economics from the year 2008.

<!-- BEGIN GENERATED: category-index — maintained by build-structure; do not edit by hand -->
| Topic | Summary |
|-------|---------|
| [Lehman Brothers Collapse](lehman-brothers-collapse.md) | Investment bank Lehman Brothers filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy on September 15, 2008 — the largest in US history — triggering global financial panic and deepening the worldwide recession. |
| [TARP Bailout](tarp-bailout.md) | President George W. Bush signed the $700 billion Troubled Asset Relief Program on October 3, 2008, authorizing the US Treasury to purchase toxic assets and stabilize the collapsing financial system. |
| [Bernie Madoff Ponzi Scheme Exposure](bernie-madoff-ponzi-scheme.md) | Bernard Madoff was arrested on December 11, 2008, after confessing to a decades-long Ponzi scheme estimated at $64.8 billion — the largest securities fraud in US history at the time. |
| [Cyclone Nargis, Myanmar (May 2008)](cyclone-nargis.md) | The category-4 cyclone struck Myanmar's Irrawaddy Delta on May 2–3, 2008, killing approximately 138,000 people and exposing the military junta's refusal to accept international disaster relief. |
| [2008 Sichuan Earthquake](sichuan-earthquake.md) | The magnitude-8.0 Wenchuan earthquake struck Sichuan, China on May 12, 2008, killing about 69,000 people and igniting a national controversy over collapsed "tofu-dreg" schools. |
| [2008 Global Financial Crisis](2008-financial-crisis.md) | The collapse of the US subprime mortgage market and the September 15, 2008 Lehman Brothers bankruptcy triggered the worst financial crisis since the Great Depression, prompting unprecedented global bailouts and central-bank intervention. |
<!-- END GENERATED: category-index -->
